Justice Prisoner and Alien Transportation System Fund, United States Marshals Service, , , 2762A–55, provided in part that:Pub. L. 106–553, § 1(a)(2) [title I]114 Stat. 2762Dec. 21, 2000“Beginning in fiscal year 2000 and thereafter, payment shall be made from the Justice Prisoner and Alien Transportation System Fund for necessary expenses related to the scheduling and transportation of United States prisoners and illegal and criminal aliens in the custody of the United States Marshals Service, as authorized in , including, without limitation, salaries and expenses, operations, and the acquisition, lease, and maintenance of aircraft and support facilities: , That the Fund shall be reimbursed or credited with advance payments from amounts available to the Department of Justice, other Federal agencies, and other sources at rates that will recover the expenses of Fund operations, including, without limitation, accrual of annual leave and depreciation of plant and equipment of the Fund: , That proceeds from the disposal of Fund aircraft shall be credited to the Fund: , That amounts in the Fund shall be available without fiscal year limitation, and may be used for operating equipment lease agreements that do not exceed 10 years.”18 U.S.C. 4013ProvidedProvided furtherProvided furtherSimilar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriations act:, , , 1501A–7.Pub. L. 106–113, div. B, § 1000(a)(1) [title I]113 Stat. 1535Nov. 29, 1999, , , 2681–54, provided that:Pub. L. 105–277, div. A, § 101(b) [title I]112 Stat. 2681–50Oct. 21, 1998“There is hereby established a Justice Prisoner and Alien Transportation System Fund for the payment of necessary expenses related to the scheduling and transportation of United States prisoners and illegal and criminal aliens in the custody of the United States Marshals Service, as authorized in , including, without limitation, salaries and expenses, operations, and the acquisition, lease, and maintenance of aircraft and support facilities: , That the Fund shall be reimbursed or credited with advance payments from amounts available to the Department of Justice, other Federal agencies, and other sources at rates that will recover the expenses of Fund operations, including, without limitation, accrual of annual leave and depreciation of plant and equipment of the Fund: , That proceeds from the disposal of Fund aircraft shall be credited to the Fund: , That amounts in the Fund shall be available without fiscal year limitation, and may be used for operating equipment lease agreements that do not exceed 5 years.”18 U.S.C. 4013ProvidedProvided furtherProvided further

children.text: necessary clothing;
medical care and necessary guard hire; and

the housing, care, and security of persons held in custody of a United States marshal pursuant to Federal law under agreements with State or local units of government or contracts with private entities.
amounts made available for purposes of this paragraph shall not exceed the average per-inmate cost of constructing similar confinement facilities for the Federal prison population,
the availability of such federally assisted facility shall be assured for housing Federal prisoners, and
the per diem rate charged for housing such Federal prisoners shall not exceed allowable costs or other conditions specified in the contract or cooperative agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of Federal detainees in the district; and

children.label: B
children.text: the availability of appropriate Federal, State, and local government detention facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: be located in a district that has been designated as needing additional Federal detention facilities pursuant to paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: meet the standards of the American Correctional Association;

children.label: C
children.text: comply with all applicable State and local laws and regulations;

children.label: D
children.text: have approved fire, security, escape, and riot plans; and

children.label: E
children.text: comply with any other regulations that the Marshals Service deems appropriate.
The United States Marshals Service shall provide an opportunity for public comment on a contract under subsection (a)(3).

children.heading: Health Care Fees For Federal Prisoners in Non-Federal Institutions.—
children.label: d Health Care Fees For Federal Prisoners in Non-Federal Institutions.—
A
children.text: the prisoner is confined in a non-Federal institution pursuant to an agreement between the Federal Government and the State or local government;

children.label: i
children.text: is authorized under State law; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not exceed the amount collected from State or local prisoners for the same services; and

children.label: i
children.text: are provided within or outside of the institution by a person who is licensed or certified under State law to provide health care services and who is operating within the scope of such license;

children.label: ii
children.text: constitute a health care visit within the meaning of ; andsection 4048(a)(4) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: are not preventative health care services, emergency services, prenatal care, diagnosis or treatment of chronic infectious diseases, mental health care, or substance abuse treatment.

children.heading: .—No refusal of treatment for financial reasons
children.label: 2 .—No refusal of treatment for financial reasons
A
children.text: the account of the prisoner is insolvent; or

children.label: B
children.text: the prisoner is otherwise unable to pay a fee assessed under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: until the expiration of the 30-day period beginning on the date on which each prisoner in the prison system is provided with such notices; and

children.label: B
children.text: for services provided before the expiration of such period.

children.heading: .—Notice to prisoners of state or local implementation
children.label: 4 .—Notice to prisoners of state or local implementation
children.text: The implementation of this subsection by the State or local government, and any amendment to that implementation, shall not take effect until the expiration of the 30-day period beginning on the date on which each prisoner in the prison system is provided with written and oral notices of the provisions of that implementation (or amendment, as the case may be). A fee under this subsection may not be assessed against, or collected from, a prisoner pursuant to such implementation (or amendments, as the case may be) for services provided before the expiration of such period.

children.heading: .—Notice before public comment period
children.label: 5 .—Notice before public comment period
children.text: Before the beginning of any period a proposed implementation under this subsection is open to public comment, written and oral notice of the provisions of that proposed implementation shall be provided to groups that advocate on behalf of Federal prisoners and to each prisoner subject to such proposed implementation.

children.heading: .—Comprehensive hiv/aids services required
children.label: 6 .—Comprehensive hiv/aids services required
children.text: Any State or local government assessing or collecting a fee under this subsection shall provide comprehensive coverage for services relating to human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) and acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS) to each Federal prisoner in the custody of such State or local government when medically appropriate. The State or local government may not assess or collect a fee under this subsection for providing such coverage.
